channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Ingazi Youth Ambassadors Cohort 2: Leadership Opportunity for Rwandan Youth (Ages 18–30)
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for Ingazi Youth Ambassadors – Cohort 2, a youth leadership initiative designed to empower young people in Rwanda to become changemakers in their communities. This program seeks motivated youth leaders who are ready to take action, inspire others, and contribute to positive social and community development across Rwanda. About the Ingazi […]

The post Ingazi Youth Ambassadors Cohort 2: Leadership Opportunity for Rwandan Youth (Ages 18–30) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for Ingazi Youth Ambassadors – Cohort 2, a youth leadership initiative designed to empower young people in Rwanda to become changemakers in their communities.

This program seeks motivated youth leaders who are ready to take action, inspire others, and contribute to positive social and community development across Rwanda.

About the Ingazi Youth Ambassadors Program

The Ingazi Youth Ambassadors program identifies and supports young leaders who are passionate about driving meaningful change at the local level.

Selected ambassadors will be part of a cohort that promotes leadership, civic engagement, and community-driven impact initiatives.

Why This Program Matters

Youth leadership is a key driver of sustainable community development. This initiative equips young people with the platform to:

  • Engage directly in community transformation
  • Develop leadership and communication skills
  • Inspire peers and contribute to local solutions
  • Participate in youth-led impact initiatives

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to:

  • Young people from Rwanda
  • Ages 18 to 30 years
  • Individuals passionate about leadership and community development
  • Youth ready to take active roles in creating local impact

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

15 May 2026

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Training Opportuinities , Visit OFY here.

The post Ingazi Youth Ambassadors Cohort 2: Leadership Opportunity for Rwandan Youth (Ages 18–30) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Advisory Committee 2026: Opportunity for Young Leaders in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Youth Advisory Committee, a pan-African initiative designed to elevate youth voices in shaping entrepreneurship and economic empowerment programs across the continent. Delivered through a partnership with the Mastercard Foundation, this initiative empowers young people to actively influence program design, ensuring that interventions better reflect the realities, challenges, and aspirations […]

The post Youth Advisory Committee 2026: Opportunity for Young Leaders in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Youth Advisory Committee, a pan-African initiative designed to elevate youth voices in shaping entrepreneurship and economic empowerment programs across the continent.

Delivered through a partnership with the Mastercard Foundation, this initiative empowers young people to actively influence program design, ensuring that interventions better reflect the realities, challenges, and aspirations of youth in Africa.

About the Youth Advisory Committee

The Youth Advisory Committee brings together young leaders from Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda to co-create solutions that strengthen youth entrepreneurship and economic inclusion programs.

Members will play a direct role in shaping initiatives that support young people to thrive, innovate, and access meaningful opportunities.

What Committee Members Will Do

Selected youth advisors will:

  • Share lived experiences and insights on youth challenges
  • Identify barriers to entrepreneurship and employment
  • Contribute to program design and decision-making processes
  • Co-create inclusive and impactful solutions
  • Support the development of youth-centered economic empowerment initiatives

Why This Opportunity Matters

Youth participation is essential in designing effective development programs. This initiative ensures that young people are not only beneficiaries but also active contributors to shaping solutions that affect their future.

By joining the committee, participants will help build more inclusive systems that improve access to entrepreneurship and economic opportunities across Africa.

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to young people from:

  • Ethiopia
  • Ghana
  • Nigeria
  • Rwanda

Applicants should:

  • Be young people with lived experience relevant to entrepreneurship or economic empowerment
  • Be passionate about youth development and inclusion
  • Be willing to contribute ideas and collaborate with others
  • Have interest in shaping programs that support young people’s economic participation

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by:

May 12, 2026

How to Apply

Apply here

For more Policy opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Youth Advisory Committee 2026: Opportunity for Young Leaders in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Re-D Fund 2026: Microgrants for Civil Society Organizations Advancing Digital Democracy in East and Southeast Asia
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Tifa Foundation has launched the Re-D Fund, a major regional funding initiative designed to strengthen digital democracy, civic participation, human rights advocacy, and organizational resilience across East and Southeast Asia. The Re-D Fund, which stands for “Reimagining Futures for Digital Democracy,” provides financial and non-financial support to civil society groups using digital technologies to […]

The post Re-D Fund 2026: Microgrants for Civil Society Organizations Advancing Digital Democracy in East and Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Tifa Foundation has launched the Re-D Fund, a major regional funding initiative designed to strengthen digital democracy, civic participation, human rights advocacy, and organizational resilience across East and Southeast Asia.

The Re-D Fund, which stands for “Reimagining Futures for Digital Democracy,” provides financial and non-financial support to civil society groups using digital technologies to address shrinking civic space and growing barriers to democratic participation in the digital era.

The initiative seeks to empower marginalized and underrepresented groups by providing microgrants and ecosystem support for projects that strengthen civic freedoms, inclusive governance, advocacy, and democratic innovation.

The programme is expected to support at least 100 local collaborators across the region, with a strong emphasis on women, youth, grassroots organizations, Indigenous Peoples, and gender minorities.

About the Re-D Fund

The Re-D Fund is a strategic grant mechanism developed to:

  • Expand civic participation through digital tools
  • Advance rights-based reforms
  • Strengthen organizational resilience
  • Support marginalized communities
  • Promote democratic innovation
  • Improve access to funding for underrepresented civil society actors

The fund responds directly to challenges such as:

  • Shrinking civic space
  • Digital repression
  • Funding inequities
  • Exclusion of grassroots organizations from traditional grant systems
  • Increasing restrictions on democratic participation

By providing accessible financial support and ecosystem-building opportunities, the Re-D Fund aims to strengthen civil society’s role in protecting democracy and human rights in the digital age.

Main Objectives of the Re-D Fund

The programme seeks to ensure that local civil society groups in East and Southeast Asia gain equitable access to:

  • Financial resources
  • Capacity-building opportunities
  • Support ecosystems
  • Advocacy platforms
  • Digital democracy tools

The ultimate goal is to enable organizations to develop and implement strategies that strengthen democratic participation and civic resilience amid digital-era challenges.

Who the Re-D Fund Supports

The Re-D Fund prioritizes support for marginalized and underrepresented groups, including:

  • Women-led organizations
  • Youth groups
  • Informal and grassroots movements
  • Indigenous Peoples
  • Gender minorities
  • Community-based advocacy groups
  • Civil society networks

At least 50% of supported subgrantees are expected to come from marginalized communities.

Eligible Organizations and Groups

Eligible applicants include:

  • Civil society groups
  • Grassroots collectives
  • Community organizations
  • Informal advocacy groups
  • Civic networks
  • Rights-based coalitions

Applicants must:

  • Operate in East or Southeast Asia
  • Work in countries categorized as closed, repressed, or obstructed by the CIVICUS Monitor
  • Be eligible for OECD DAC Official Development Assistance
  • Have operated for at least 3 years
  • Be legally registered or partnered with a fiscal sponsor
  • Be capable of receiving grant funds directly or via a host organization

Priority Countries

The Re-D Fund specifically prioritizes projects implemented in:

  • Cambodia
  • Indonesia
  • Laos
  • Malaysia
  • Myanmar
  • Philippines
  • Taiwan
  • Thailand
  • Timor-Leste
  • Vietnam

Thematic Focus Areas

Projects funded by the Re-D Fund must address civic space and democracy in the digital era through one or more thematic tracks.

1. Advancing Reforms for Civic Freedoms

This thematic area supports:

  • Legal reforms
  • Civic freedoms advocacy
  • Democratic governance innovation
  • Policy development
  • Human rights protections

Eligible activities include:

  • Legal research
  • Policy research
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Human rights monitoring
  • Transparency initiatives
  • Policy dialogue forums
  • Democratic innovation activities

2. Enhancing Civic Participation of Marginalized Groups

This focus area supports initiatives that:

  • Increase participation of excluded communities
  • Strengthen digital inclusion
  • Promote equitable civic engagement
  • Amplify marginalized voices

3. Strengthening Civic Influence

Projects under this category may include:

  • Public engagement strategies
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Community mobilization
  • Civic technology solutions
  • Coalition-building efforts

4. Fostering Holistic Resilience

The programme supports organizational resilience through:

  • Digital security
  • Operational sustainability
  • Community resilience models
  • Crisis preparedness
  • Organizational strengthening

5. Expanding Civil Society Resources

This thematic track focuses on:

  • Capacity building
  • Resource-sharing systems
  • Infrastructure development
  • Digital ecosystem support
  • Long-term sustainability

Grant Structure

The Re-D Fund will support:

  • Two cohorts of subgrantees
  • Approximately 50 subgrantees per cohort

Cohort I

Project implementation period:

  • March 1 – May 31, 2026

Status:

  • Open

Cohort II

Project implementation period:

  • July 22 – October 23, 2026

Status:

  • Open

Application Deadline

Applications for Cohort II must be submitted before:

June 5, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all eligibility and thematic requirements.

Read about the Download Guidelines and APPLY.

Grant Amounts

The Re-D Fund provides two funding categories.

Single Applications

Applicants may receive up to:

  • USD 5,000

Joint Applications (2+ Organizations)

Collaborative applications may receive up to:

  • USD 10,000

The joint application category encourages partnerships, coalition-building, and collaborative democratic innovation efforts.

Why the Re-D Fund Matters

Civil society organizations across East and Southeast Asia increasingly face:

  • Political restrictions
  • Funding barriers
  • Digital surveillance
  • Shrinking civic space
  • Exclusion from decision-making processes

The Re-D Fund addresses these challenges by supporting grassroots organizations that often struggle to access traditional funding systems.

The initiative recognizes that marginalized communities are frequently the most affected by democratic decline and digital inequality, yet remain among the least funded.

Key Benefits of the Re-D Fund

Selected organizations benefit from:

  • Direct financial support
  • Increased visibility
  • Ecosystem networking opportunities
  • Support for digital democracy initiatives
  • Access to collaborative regional partnerships
  • Flexible support for innovative civic solutions

What Makes This Fund Unique?

The Re-D Fund stands out because it:

  • Prioritizes marginalized communities
  • Supports grassroots and informal groups
  • Focuses on digital democracy
  • Encourages collaborative applications
  • Combines financial and non-financial support
  • Emphasizes feminist, inclusive, and rights-based approaches

About Tifa Foundation

The Tifa Foundation is an Indonesian organization that promotes:

  • Openness
  • Diversity
  • Equality
  • Justice
  • Democratic participation

The organization works through:

  • Strategic multi-stakeholder collaboration
  • Local and international partnerships
  • Constructive engagement approaches
  • Resource provision and direct implementation

Tifa Foundation plays a critical role in advancing civic participation and democratic resilience across the region.

Click HERE to learn more about The Tifa Foundation’s Re-D Fund 2026.

Official Resources

Applicants are encouraged to:

  • Download the official guidelines
  • Review the FAQ section
  • Attend the webinar
  • Carefully prepare application materials

Discover more global Grant Opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Re-D Fund 2026: Microgrants for Civil Society Organizations Advancing Digital Democracy in East and Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Eco Champions Germany 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Environmental Changemakers Living in Germany
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The SOS Children’s Villages has officially launched applications for the 2026 Eco Champions Germany Program, an intensive 1.5-year climate leadership and environmental justice initiative designed to empower young people who are passionate about climate action, sustainability, advocacy, and community-driven environmental solutions. The programme seeks five motivated young leaders aged 16–26 who are living in Germany […]

The post Eco Champions Germany 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Environmental Changemakers Living in Germany appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The SOS Children’s Villages has officially launched applications for the 2026 Eco Champions Germany Program, an intensive 1.5-year climate leadership and environmental justice initiative designed to empower young people who are passionate about climate action, sustainability, advocacy, and community-driven environmental solutions.

The programme seeks five motivated young leaders aged 16–26 who are living in Germany and committed to addressing climate change through activism, innovation, awareness-building, or policy engagement.

Through personalized mentoring, financial support, networking opportunities, and international exposure, Eco Champions Germany aims to equip youth with the resources and confidence needed to implement impactful climate-related initiatives within their communities and beyond.

Participants will also gain the opportunity to attend a Global Summit in Nigeria and connect with young climate activists from around the world.

About the Eco Champions Germany Program

Eco Champions Germany is a youth-centered climate and sustainability initiative organized by SOS Children’s Villages. The programme supports young people in:

  • Launching climate-related initiatives
  • Expanding existing environmental projects
  • Advocating for climate justice
  • Building sustainable community solutions
  • Strengthening youth leadership in environmental policy and activism

The programme runs for approximately 18 months and combines mentorship, project acceleration, training, financial support, and global collaboration.

Main Goals of the Program

The Eco Champions initiative seeks to:

  • Empower youth-led environmental action
  • Promote climate justice and sustainability
  • Support marginalized and underrepresented youth
  • Strengthen climate advocacy and leadership
  • Build innovative local solutions to environmental challenges
  • Encourage youth participation in policy and decision-making

What Participants Will Receive

Selected Eco Champions will benefit from a comprehensive support system designed to help them transform ideas into impactful environmental action.

1. Monthly Seed Funding

Participants receive:

  • €300 monthly seed funding

This funding supports the implementation and development of participants’ climate and sustainability projects throughout the programme period.

2. Individual Mentoring and Coaching

Participants gain access to:

  • Personalized mentoring
  • Leadership guidance
  • Project development support
  • Advocacy coaching

3. Professional Training

The programme includes:

  • Workshops
  • Skills development sessions
  • Advocacy training
  • Project management learning opportunities

4. International Networking Opportunities

Eco Champions will:

  • Connect with young climate activists globally
  • Engage with international sustainability networks
  • Participate in exchange programmes and conferences

5. Global Summit in Nigeria

One of the major highlights of the programme is participation in the:

  • Global Summit in Nigeria
  • Scheduled for September 21–27, 2026

At the summit, participants will meet Eco Champions from other countries and collaborate on global climate discussions.

6. Collaboration Opportunities with Companies

Participants may collaborate with companies within the SOS Children’s Villages network to support their initiatives and increase project visibility.

7. Opportunities to Influence Decision-Makers

The programme provides:

  • Platforms for youth advocacy
  • Opportunities to engage policymakers
  • Access to national and international climate discussions

Who Can Apply?

The programme is open to:

  • Young people aged 16–26
  • Individuals residing in Germany
  • Youth interested in environmental justice and sustainability
  • Young people with project ideas or ongoing initiatives
  • Climate activists and policy advocates

Applicants should be able to:

  • Communicate in English
  • Participate actively for approximately 5–10 hours monthly
  • Attend workshops and programme activities
  • Participate in the Nigeria Global Summit

Priority Groups Encouraged to Apply

The programme strongly encourages applications from:

  • Young women and girls
  • Queer youth
  • Refugees and migrants
  • Young people experiencing racism
  • Youth from low-income households
  • Individuals with disabilities
  • Young people without stable family support
  • Youth from non-academic households
  • Young people from structurally disadvantaged regions
  • Other marginalized or underrepresented communities

Do you need help with your application?

Then register here for the online workshop!

Focus Areas for Climate-Related Projects

The programme accepts a broad range of environmental initiatives including:

Climate Protection Projects

Examples include:

  • CO₂ reduction initiatives
  • Sustainability innovation projects
  • Renewable energy awareness

Environmental Education

Projects focused on:

  • Climate literacy
  • Awareness campaigns
  • Youth environmental education

Advocacy and Policy Engagement

Including:

  • Climate activism
  • Political advocacy
  • Policy awareness campaigns

Community Engagement

Projects that:

  • Mobilize communities
  • Encourage youth participation
  • Build sustainable local solutions

Programme Timeline

Application Opens

April 20, 2026

Application Deadline

May 20, 2026

Online Interviews

Until June 17, 2026

Participant Notification

By June 24, 2026

Programme Start

July 1, 2026

Global Summit in Nigeria

September 21–27, 2026

Programme Duration

18 months (1.5 years)

Program Structure

Phase 1: Introduction and Preparation

July – September 2026

  • Orientation sessions
  • Community-building meetings
  • Global Summit preparation

Phase 2: Global Summit

September 2026

  • International collaboration
  • Networking with global Eco Champions
  • Climate leadership engagement

Phase 3: Mentoring and Accelerator Phase

From October 2026 onward

  • Project implementation support
  • Continuous mentoring
  • Funding support
  • Advocacy development

The Program’s Inclusive and Anti-Discrimination Approach

Eco Champions Germany emphasizes:

  • Feminist principles
  • Anti-racist values
  • Inclusion and accessibility
  • Respectful collaboration
  • Safe participation spaces

The programme actively works to reduce participation barriers and accommodate diverse realities and support needs.

Click HERE to start your application!

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

FAQ 1: Do participants need to pay to apply or participate?

No. The application and participation are completely free. All programme-related costs, including training, mentoring, exchange activities, and the Global Summit, are fully covered.

FAQ 2: How much funding do participants receive?

Participants receive:

  • €300 per month in seed funding

to support their climate and sustainability projects throughout the programme duration.

FAQ 3: Is the programme flexible for students or working youth?

Yes. The programme aims to remain flexible and supportive of participants balancing:

  • Education
  • Employment
  • Care responsibilities
  • Other commitments

FAQ 4: What kinds of projects qualify?

Eligible initiatives may include:

  • Climate awareness campaigns
  • Advocacy work
  • Sustainability innovations
  • Educational projects
  • Environmental activism
  • Community engagement programmes

FAQ 5: Are online interviews required?

Yes. Shortlisted applicants will participate in online interviews. The organization also offers support for applicants experiencing internet or device challenges.

FAQ 6: Who evaluates the applications?

Applications are reviewed by a team of SOS Children’s Villages staff members from around the world.

FAQ 7: What selection criteria are used?

Applications are assessed based on:

  • Personal story
  • Commitment to climate action
  • Community relevance
  • Leadership potential
  • Previous impact
  • Originality and innovation

Why This Program Matters

Eco Champions Germany goes beyond traditional youth programmes by providing:

  • Long-term support
  • Funding access
  • International exposure
  • Leadership development
  • Advocacy opportunities
  • Climate justice engagement

The initiative recognizes that young people are essential drivers of environmental change and seeks to amplify their voices and solutions.

Learn more about the Climate Leadership Program for Young Environmental Changemakers Living in Germany HERE.

Official Contact Information

For questions about the programme, applicants may contact:

ecochampions@sos-kd.org

Discover more global Fellowship and Climate Action opportunities on OFY.

The post Eco Champions Germany 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Environmental Changemakers Living in Germany appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth for COP 2026 Program Opens Applications: Join a Global Climate Learning Journey for Young Changemakers
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The third edition of the Youth for COP Program is officially live, offering young people around the world an exciting opportunity to explore global climate conversations, climate diplomacy, and international negotiations connected to the Conference of Parties (COP). Designed as a dynamic climate education and engagement platform, Youth for COP 2026 empowers young people with […]

The post Youth for COP 2026 Program Opens Applications: Join a Global Climate Learning Journey for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The third edition of the Youth for COP Program is officially live, offering young people around the world an exciting opportunity to explore global climate conversations, climate diplomacy, and international negotiations connected to the Conference of Parties (COP).

Designed as a dynamic climate education and engagement platform, Youth for COP 2026 empowers young people with the knowledge, skills, networks, and confidence needed to meaningfully engage in climate action and policy discussions at local, regional, and global levels.

The program combines expert-led learning sessions, interactive workshops, online modules, simulations, and networking opportunities to help participants understand how global climate systems work and how youth voices can contribute to shaping climate solutions.

Most importantly, the program is completely free of cost and provides selected participants with certificates and opportunities to explore and engage with COP31 and the wider global climate ecosystem.

What is Youth for COP 2026?

Youth for COP is an international climate engagement initiative created to bridge the gap between young people and global climate policymaking.

Since launching in 2023, the program has evolved into a vibrant youth platform bringing together climate advocates, students, young professionals, researchers, and emerging leaders from across the world.

Now entering its third edition, Youth for COP continues to expand its impact by helping participants:

  • Understand international climate negotiations
  • Learn how COP processes work
  • Build climate communication skills
  • Develop digital advocacy capacity
  • Connect with a global network of young climate leaders

The initiative is especially valuable for young people interested in climate policy, sustainability, environmental justice, advocacy, diplomacy, youth participation, and global governance.

Why Youth for COP Matters

Climate change is one of the defining challenges of our generation, yet many young people still struggle to access spaces where climate policies and negotiations are discussed.

Youth for COP addresses this gap by creating accessible learning opportunities that:

  • Simplify complex climate negotiations
  • Build climate literacy
  • Encourage youth participation
  • Strengthen youth voices in climate spaces
  • Foster collaboration across countries and cultures

The program recognizes that meaningful climate action requires informed and empowered young leaders who can engage critically and constructively in global conversations.

What Participants Will Learn

The program offers a broad and immersive learning experience focused on international climate governance and practical advocacy skills.

Key Learning Areas Include:

1. Understanding COP and Climate Negotiations

Participants will learn:

  • What COP is
  • How UN climate negotiations work
  • How climate agreements are shaped
  • The role of governments, civil society, and youth in climate discussions

2. Climate Policy and Global Governance

The program explores:

  • International climate frameworks
  • Climate justice
  • Adaptation and mitigation
  • Loss and damage discussions
  • Global climate priorities

3. Communication and Digital Advocacy

Participants will develop:

  • Public communication skills
  • Climate storytelling techniques
  • Digital advocacy strategies
  • Social media engagement skills

4. Youth Leadership and Networking

The initiative also focuses on:

  • Leadership development
  • Collaborative learning
  • Cross-cultural engagement
  • Networking with climate-focused youth globally

Program Structure and Flow

The Youth for COP 2026 program follows a structured learning journey designed to progressively build participants’ understanding and engagement.

Step 1: Registration and Application

Interested participants must:

  • Register for the program
  • Complete the application form

Because seats are limited, participants are selected through a competitive process.

Step 2: Knowledge Building

Selected participants gain access to:

  • Expert-led sessions
  • Online learning modules
  • Climate policy discussions
  • Educational resources

This stage provides foundational understanding of climate negotiations and COP processes.

Step 3: Interactive Workshops

Participants engage in:

  • Simulations
  • Interactive activities
  • Group discussions
  • Collaborative exercises

These workshops help participants apply their knowledge in practical ways.

Step 4: Activities and Practical Engagement

Participants complete:

  • Short assignments
  • Advocacy activities
  • Communication tasks
  • Learning reflections

These exercises strengthen real-world engagement and application.

Step 5: Certification

Participants who successfully complete the program receive:

  • Certificate of participation
  • Certificate of appreciation

The certification recognizes participants’ engagement and commitment to climate learning and advocacy.

Benefits of Joining Youth for COP 2026

Participants in the program gain access to multiple educational and networking opportunities.

1. Learn Directly From Experts

The program features:

  • Climate experts
  • Policy professionals
  • Researchers
  • Youth leaders
  • Climate advocates

Participants gain valuable insights into real-world climate negotiations and global policy discussions.

2. Access Global Opportunities

Participants can explore:

  • International climate opportunities
  • Youth climate networks
  • Advocacy platforms
  • Engagement opportunities linked to COP31

3. Build Valuable Skills

The program strengthens:

  • Communication skills
  • Leadership abilities
  • Digital advocacy
  • Critical thinking
  • Climate literacy

4. Join a Global Community

Participants become part of an international network of young people passionate about climate action and sustainability.

Program Reach and Impact

Since its launch, Youth for COP has built an impressive global community.

Program Statistics

  • Over 3,600 registered participants
  • Participants from 48+ countries
  • More than 40 speakers
  • Collaboration with 25 youth organizations
  • Over 80 climate blogs published
  • More than 350 social media posts created

These numbers reflect the growing influence and global reach of the initiative.

Participant Voices and Climate Storytelling

One of the program’s unique features is its emphasis on youth storytelling and lived experiences.

Participants publish blogs exploring:

  • Climate vulnerability
  • Indigenous knowledge
  • Climate justice
  • Sustainability
  • Fashion and climate
  • Community resilience
  • Adaptation and survival

These stories amplify youth voices from regions heavily impacted by climate change and contribute to global climate dialogue.

Who Can Apply?

The program welcomes applications from young people interested in:

  • Climate action
  • Sustainability
  • Climate governance
  • Environmental advocacy
  • Policy engagement
  • Youth leadership

Importantly:

  • No prior climate experience is required
  • Students and working professionals can apply
  • Participants from different academic and professional backgrounds are encouraged

Program Format

The Youth for COP program is designed to be flexible and accessible.

Program Features

  • Online learning format
  • Interactive workshops
  • Virtual engagement
  • Flexible participation for students and professionals

This structure allows participants from different countries and time zones to engage effectively.

Cost of Participation

One of the most attractive aspects of the program is that it is:

  • Completely free of cost

This ensures accessibility for participants from diverse economic backgrounds.

Will Participants Attend COP?

While participation in the program does not automatically guarantee attendance at COP, the initiative helps participants:

  • Understand global climate spaces
  • Build climate engagement skills
  • Access opportunities connected to COP31 and related climate platforms

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Register through the official platform
  2. Complete the application form
  3. Submit all required information before seats are filled

Because participation is limited, early applications are highly encouraged.

Register HERE

Why You Should Apply

Youth for COP 2026 is more than an educational program—it is a platform for young people to become informed climate advocates and active participants in shaping global climate conversations.

Whether you are new to climate advocacy or already active in sustainability work, the program offers a valuable opportunity to:

  • Expand your knowledge
  • Strengthen your voice
  • Build international networks
  • Develop practical advocacy skills
  • Engage with global climate conversations in meaningful ways

For young changemakers passionate about climate action, Youth for COP 2026 could be a transformative experience.

For more information about this opportunity, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post Youth for COP 2026 Program Opens Applications: Join a Global Climate Learning Journey for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026: Australia Opens Up to $100,000 Funding for Hands-On STEM Learning Projects
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Organizations across Australia now have an exciting opportunity to secure significant funding through the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026, a national initiative designed to strengthen creativity, innovation, and hands-on STEM learning among young people. The program provides grants ranging from $20,000 to $100,000 to support community-based STEM projects that help students and youth […]

The post Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026: Australia Opens Up to $100,000 Funding for Hands-On STEM Learning Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations across Australia now have an exciting opportunity to secure significant funding through the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026, a national initiative designed to strengthen creativity, innovation, and hands-on STEM learning among young people.

The program provides grants ranging from $20,000 to $100,000 to support community-based STEM projects that help students and youth under 18 years old develop practical skills in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics through inquiry-based and experiential learning activities.

The initiative particularly focuses on increasing participation among groups that have historically been underrepresented in STEM education and careers, ensuring more young people across Australia gain access to meaningful STEM opportunities.

About the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026

The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants are part of Australia’s broader effort to promote innovation, scientific literacy, and technological capability among young people.

The grants support organizations that deliver engaging STEM activities outside traditional classroom settings. These projects encourage experimentation, creativity, problem-solving, design thinking, engineering skills, and entrepreneurship through interactive and hands-on experiences.

Rather than relying solely on theoretical instruction, the program emphasizes practical learning approaches where students actively build, create, test, and explore STEM concepts.

Funding Amount Available

Successful applicants can receive:

  • Grants from $20,000 to $100,000

The funding is intended to help organizations design and deliver impactful STEM engagement initiatives for young people under 18 years of age.

Application Deadline

Applications for the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026 are currently open.

Closing Date

  • Wednesday, 17 June 2026
  • Time: 5:00 PM AEST (Australian Eastern Standard Time)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early and ensure all required supporting documentation is complete before submission.

Purpose of the Program

The Maker Projects Grants aim to:

  • Foster creativity and inquiry-based learning
  • Improve STEM knowledge and practical skills
  • Increase access to STEM opportunities
  • Encourage innovation and entrepreneurial thinking
  • Inspire young people to pursue future STEM studies and careers

The program recognizes the growing importance of STEM education in preparing future generations for evolving industries, digital transformation, and technological advancement.

Key Objectives of the Grant Opportunity

The program has several important objectives designed to improve STEM participation and educational outcomes among young Australians.

1. Deliver Hands-On STEM Learning

Projects should allow students and youth under 18 years old to:

  • Experiment with technologies
  • Explore engineering and programming concepts
  • Participate in maker activities
  • Develop practical STEM skills

The focus is on active participation and experiential learning.

2. Encourage Creativity and Innovation

The program supports initiatives that:

  • Promote creative problem-solving
  • Encourage entrepreneurial thinking
  • Build confidence in STEM-related activities
  • Inspire innovation through practical engagement

3. Increase Interest in STEM Careers

Projects should help participants:

  • Discover future career pathways
  • Build interest in STEM subjects
  • Understand real-world applications of technology and science

This long-term approach helps strengthen Australia’s future STEM workforce.

4. Improve Accessibility and Inclusion

A major focus of the grants is ensuring STEM opportunities are accessible to young people who may face barriers to participation.

The program particularly supports projects serving:

  • Regional communities
  • Rural areas
  • Remote communities
  • Economically disadvantaged groups

Priority Groups Supported by the Program

The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants place special emphasis on increasing participation among groups historically underrepresented in STEM.

These include:

1. Women and Girls

Projects that encourage female participation in STEM learning and leadership are strongly supported.

2. First Nations Peoples

Programs that engage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people are encouraged.

3. Young People in Regional, Rural, and Remote Areas

The grants seek to improve STEM access outside major metropolitan areas.

4. Culturally and Linguistically Diverse (CALD) Communities

Projects supporting inclusive STEM participation among multicultural communities are welcomed.

5. People with Disability

Inclusive STEM learning opportunities for young people with disabilities are prioritized.

6. Young People from Low Socio-Economic or Educationally Disadvantaged Backgrounds

The program supports initiatives that reduce barriers and create equal learning opportunities.

Expected Outcomes of the Program

The Maker Projects Grants aim to generate long-term positive outcomes for young people and communities across Australia.

Anticipated Outcomes Include:

  • Increased STEM knowledge and practical skills
  • Greater participation in STEM-related activities
  • Improved innovation and entrepreneurial thinking
  • Increased accessibility to quality STEM learning experiences
  • Stronger engagement with science and technology pathways
  • Long-term development of inquiry-based learning skills

Who Can Apply?

Organizations may apply if they meet the eligibility criteria outlined in the grant guidelines.

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Eligibility as an approved entity
  • An eligible STEM project
  • Eligible project expenditure

The complete eligibility requirements are detailed in the official grant opportunity guidelines.

Eligible Projects

Projects funded under this program should:

  • Deliver community-based STEM engagement activities
  • Target students and youth under 18 years old
  • Use hands-on and inquiry-based learning approaches
  • Encourage experimentation and practical skill development

Examples may include:

  • STEM workshops
  • Coding and robotics programs
  • Maker spaces
  • Engineering challenges
  • Innovation labs
  • Technology camps
  • Science engagement events

How to Apply

Organizations interested in applying should carefully prepare before starting the application process.

Application Process

Step 1: Review the Grant Guidelines

Applicants should thoroughly read and understand:

  • Eligibility rules
  • Funding conditions
  • Assessment criteria
  • Project requirements

Step 2: Create or Log Into the Online Portal

Applications must be submitted through the official online grants portal.

Step 3: Complete the Application

Applicants should provide:

  • Detailed project information
  • Supporting evidence
  • Budget details
  • Impact goals
  • Organizational information

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications must be fully completed before submission.

Incomplete or incorrect applications may not be considered, and mistakes cannot be corrected after submission.

Apply HERE

How Applications Are Assessed

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Alignment with program objectives
  • Potential impact on young people
  • Accessibility and inclusion
  • Project quality and feasibility
  • Community engagement
  • Value for money

Strong applications will clearly demonstrate how the project will create meaningful STEM learning outcomes.

Why These Grants Matter

STEM education is increasingly important in today’s rapidly evolving world. However, many young people still face barriers to accessing quality STEM learning opportunities.

The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants help bridge this gap by supporting organizations that create accessible, engaging, and practical STEM experiences for diverse communities across Australia.

By investing in youth STEM engagement today, the program contributes to:

  • Future workforce development
  • Innovation ecosystems
  • Educational equity
  • Economic growth
  • Community empowerment

Final Thoughts

The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026 offer a valuable opportunity for organizations passionate about STEM education to expand their impact and empower the next generation of innovators, creators, and problem-solvers.

With funding of up to $100,000 available, eligible organizations can design transformative projects that inspire curiosity, creativity, and long-term engagement in science and technology.

Organizations working to make STEM education more inclusive, practical, and accessible are strongly encouraged to apply before the June 17, 2026 deadline.

 For more information about the opportunity, visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026: Australia Opens Up to $100,000 Funding for Hands-On STEM Learning Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Black Girls Defense Fund – Resistance and Resilience Grant 2026 (Up to $20,000 Funding Opportunity for Southern US Organizations)
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Black Girls Defense Fund has officially opened applications for its Resistance and Resilience Grant, a powerful funding opportunity aimed at strengthening organizations that defend, protect, and advocate for Black girls, women, and femme-identifying youth in the Southern United States. This grant supports community-led initiatives working at the frontlines of justice, safety, health equity, and […]

The post Black Girls Defense Fund – Resistance and Resilience Grant 2026 (Up to $20,000 Funding Opportunity for Southern US Organizations) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Black Girls Defense Fund has officially opened applications for its Resistance and Resilience Grant, a powerful funding opportunity aimed at strengthening organizations that defend, protect, and advocate for Black girls, women, and femme-identifying youth in the Southern United States.

This grant supports community-led initiatives working at the frontlines of justice, safety, health equity, and systemic change. It is designed to fund organizations that are actively addressing structural issues such as gender-based violence, reproductive health inequities, adultification, dehumanization, and harms caused by carceral systems.

Grant funding ranges from $5,000 to $20,000, with a strong emphasis on supporting grassroots movements and organizations deeply rooted in their communities.

About the Black Girls Defense Fund

The Black Girls Defense Fund is an initiative of the Southern Black Girls and Women’s Consortium. It exists to strengthen and protect movements advancing the rights, dignity, and safety of Black girls and women across the Southern United States.

The fund focuses on:

  • Community protection and safety
  • Reproductive and maternal health justice
  • Ending gender-based violence
  • Restorative justice approaches
  • Challenging systemic dehumanization and adultification

The initiative prioritizes organizations that are led by and directly accountable to the communities they serve.

About the Grant

The Resistance and Resilience Grant is a flexible funding opportunity designed to support both new and existing initiatives.

It provides general operating support to help organizations sustain and expand their work in areas including:

  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Community programs
  • Justice reform initiatives
  • Health and safety programs
  • Youth empowerment efforts

The goal is to strengthen long-term capacity for organizations working with Black girls, women, and femme-identifying youth.

Funding Amount

  • Minimum: $5,000
  • Maximum: $20,000

This funding can be used for general operating support, allowing organizations flexibility in how resources are allocated.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for this grant, applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

Organizational Requirements

  • Must be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, OR
  • Have a fiscal sponsor with a nonprofit organization

Budget Requirement

  • Organization must have an annual budget under $1 million

Geographic Focus

Applicants must operate in at least one of the following Southern U.S. states:

  • Alabama
  • Arkansas
  • Florida
  • Georgia
  • Kentucky
  • Louisiana
  • Mississippi
  • North Carolina
  • South Carolina
  • Tennessee
  • Virginia
  • West Virginia
  • Texas

Eligible Focus Areas

Organizations must be working on initiatives that address at least one of the following:

  • Gender-based violence prevention and response
  • Reproductive and maternal health justice
  • Justice system and carceral system reform
  • Restorative justice practices
  • Safety and wellbeing of Black girls and women
  • Advocacy against adultification and dehumanization

Key Responsibilities of Funded Work

Funded organizations are expected to demonstrate impact through:

Program Implementation

  • Designing or continuing initiatives that support Black girls, women, and femme youth
  • Implementing advocacy or direct service programs
  • Strengthening community safety systems

Community Engagement

  • Engaging directly with affected communities
  • Incorporating feedback from stakeholders
  • Ensuring culturally responsive programming

Impact Measurement

  • Defining clear outcomes and success indicators
  • Tracking program effectiveness and community impact
  • Reporting on progress and learnings

Application Requirements

Applicants must complete the JustFund Common Application™ and submit supporting documents.

Required Documents

  • W9 form
  • Proof of 501(c)(3) status OR fiscal sponsorship agreement
  • Most recent Form 990
  • Organizational budget

Application Questions Overview

Applicants will be required to respond to detailed questions covering:

1. Program Design and Impact

  • Description of the initiative
  • Activities and implementation plan
  • Expected outcomes and metrics
  • Organizational background and leadership history

2. Community Engagement Strategy

  • How community input is collected and integrated
  • How programs are sustained or expanded
  • Media engagement preferences

3. Resource Allocation

  • Use of grant funding
  • Partnerships and collaborations
  • Funding gaps and sustainability strategy
  • Additional support needs beyond funding

Key Dates and Timeline

  • Application Opens: May 5, 2026 (9:00 AM EST)
  • Information Session: May 7, 2026 (7:00 PM EST)
  • Application Deadline: May 14, 2026 (8:00 PM EST)
  • Funding Decisions Announced: June 24, 2026

Information Session

A live workshop will be held to guide applicants through the process and answer questions.

Details:

  • Date: May 7, 2026
  • Time: 7:00 PM Eastern Time (US & Canada)

How to Apply

Click here for more, to register and to apply

Important notes:

  • Account activation may take up to 2 days
  • Applicants are encouraged to apply at least 3 days before the deadline
  • Late submissions may not be considered

Why This Grant Matters

This funding opportunity plays a critical role in strengthening grassroots movements that protect and empower Black girls and women in the Southern United States. It provides essential unrestricted support that allows organizations to:

  • Sustain vital advocacy work
  • Respond to community needs in real time
  • Expand justice-focused initiatives
  • Strengthen leadership in marginalized communities

For more global grant opportunities Click here

The post Black Girls Defense Fund – Resistance and Resilience Grant 2026 (Up to $20,000 Funding Opportunity for Southern US Organizations) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: YLabs Youth Researcher Opportunity in Nigeria 2026 – Remote Field-Based Research Role for Early-Career Professionals
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
YLabs is inviting applications from Nigeria-based early-career professionals for an exciting Youth Researcher opportunity focused on participatory and community-based research. This short-term contractor role offers candidates the chance to contribute to meaningful youth-centered projects while gaining hands-on experience in ethnographic research, design research, and field engagement. The organization is looking for individuals who are deeply […]

The post YLabs Youth Researcher Opportunity in Nigeria 2026 – Remote Field-Based Research Role for Early-Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
YLabs is inviting applications from Nigeria-based early-career professionals for an exciting Youth Researcher opportunity focused on participatory and community-based research. This short-term contractor role offers candidates the chance to contribute to meaningful youth-centered projects while gaining hands-on experience in ethnographic research, design research, and field engagement.

The organization is looking for individuals who are deeply connected to their communities and capable of gathering rich, context-driven insights through direct interaction with young people and local stakeholders.

This role is ideal for emerging professionals interested in:

  • Community engagement
  • Participatory research
  • Youth development
  • Social impact
  • Human-centered design
  • Public health and development research

Successful applicants will support field research activities across Nigeria while collaborating remotely with a global multidisciplinary team.

About YLabs

YLabs is a globally recognized design and research organization dedicated to amplifying youth power, agency, and opportunity through innovative technologies and inclusive research methods.

Founded in 2014 at the Harvard Innovation Lab, YLabs partners directly with young people to co-create digital products and services that address pressing social challenges affecting youth globally.

The organization works across more than 20 countries and focuses on:

  • Youth health and wellness
  • Economic opportunity
  • Climate resilience
  • Digital innovation
  • Behavioral science
  • Adolescent development

YLabs combines expertise in:

  • Human-centered design
  • Epidemiology
  • Digital product design
  • Community engagement
  • Research and behavioral science

The organization is headquartered in Rwanda and the United States, with a globally distributed workforce.

About the Youth Researcher Role

The Youth Researcher role is designed for individuals with foundational experience in research and field engagement who can work directly within communities to uncover lived experiences, patterns, challenges, and opportunities.

Researchers will support participatory research activities by:

  • Conducting interviews
  • Leading group discussions
  • Carrying out observations
  • Documenting field insights
  • Participating in synthesis sessions

The role requires a strong understanding of local contexts and the ability to respectfully engage individuals from diverse backgrounds.

Contract Duration and Work Structure

Employment Type

  • Independent Contractor

Work Location

  • Nigeria (Remote with field-based activities)

Contract Timeline

  • Expected to run from May to Mid-July 2026

Work Style

  • Primarily field-based
  • Some remote collaboration required

Key Responsibilities

Selected Youth Researchers will support a range of participatory research activities.

Core Duties Include:

1. Field Research Coordination

  • Plan and coordinate field visits
  • Manage schedules and local logistics
  • Navigate community engagement processes

2. Conducting Research Activities

  • Carry out one-on-one interviews
  • Facilitate group discussions
  • Conduct direct field observations

3. Community Engagement

  • Build trust with youth and community stakeholders
  • Create safe and respectful environments for discussion
  • Encourage honest and open participation

4. Documentation and Insight Gathering

  • Capture detailed field notes
  • Record reflections and observations using structured tools
  • Identify behavioral and environmental patterns

5. Data Reflection and Synthesis

  • Participate in debrief sessions
  • Surface emerging themes and recurring issues
  • Help identify opportunities and tensions within communities

6. Ethical and Safe Research Practice

  • Follow safeguarding and child protection guidelines
  • Handle sensitive information responsibly
  • Adhere to ethical research standards

Ideal Candidate Profile

YLabs is looking for candidates who are:

  • Community-centered
  • Curious and empathetic
  • Strong listeners
  • Respectful and culturally aware
  • Reflective and open to learning

Applicants should be able to connect authentically with people while maintaining professionalism and ethical standards.

Required Qualifications and Experience

Minimum Requirements

Candidates should have:

  • At least 2 years of experience in:
    • Research
    • Monitoring and evaluation
    • Community engagement
    • Design research
    • Related development or social impact work

This experience may come from:

  • Education
  • Professional work
  • Training
  • Community or lived experience

Required Skills

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Strong communication skills in English and relevant local languages
  • Ability to conduct interviews and document findings clearly
  • Strong observation and note-taking skills
  • Comfort interacting with diverse community groups
  • Ability to adapt to dynamic field environments
  • Familiarity with digital tools and online collaboration platforms
  • Confidential handling of information
  • Understanding of ethical research and safeguarding principles

Additional Advantage

Experience working with:

  • Major funders
  • Philanthropic organizations
  • Development organizations across Africa

will be considered an added advantage.

Compensation and Benefits

Pay Rate

  • Up to $11 per hour
  • Compensation is based on experience

Payment Method

  • Monthly bank transfer payments

YLabs follows Project Fair compensation principles, ensuring equitable pay standards based on work value and regional benchmarks.

Eligibility Criteria

This opportunity is specifically open to:

  • Candidates based in Nigeria
  • Individuals with strong understanding of local cultures and languages
  • Applicants comfortable conducting in-person field engagement

YLabs strongly encourages applications from:

  • Women
  • Persons living with disabilities

Application Requirements

Interested applicants must submit:

Required Documents

  1. Resume/CV
  2. Short video introduction (1–2 minutes)

What to Include in the Video

The video should:

  • Introduce your background
  • Explain your connection to your community
  • Discuss a community issue you care about
  • Describe how you would investigate and understand the issue
  • Explain:
    • Who you would speak with
    • How you would engage people
    • What methods you would use to analyze information

How to Submit the Video

Applicants must:

  1. Upload the video to:
    • Google Drive OR
    • YouTube
  2. Ensure access settings are:
    • “Anyone with the link can view” OR
    • “Public”
  3. Paste the video link into the application form

Important:

  • Ensure the link works before submission
  • Videos with restricted access cannot be reviewed

YLabs emphasizes that production quality is not important. Applicants are encouraged to focus on:

  • Clarity
  • Organization
  • Authenticity

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply is:

15 May 2026

Late or incomplete applications may not be considered.

Click here for more and to apply

Selection Process

YLabs will:

  • Review all submitted applications
  • Contact shortlisted candidates during the hiring process
  • Conduct background checks and criminal record clearances before final hiring decisions

All applicant information will remain confidential.

Equal Opportunity Statement

YLabs is committed to promoting equity, inclusion, and belonging in the workplace.

The organization welcomes applicants from all:

  • Cultural backgrounds
  • Educational backgrounds
  • Socio-economic groups
  • Gender identities
  • Physical abilities
  • Age groups

YLabs maintains a strict non-discrimination policy.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This opportunity provides emerging researchers in Nigeria with:

  • Real-world field research experience
  • Exposure to global development work
  • Experience in participatory and youth-centered design
  • Collaboration with international experts
  • Skills in ethnography and community engagement

It is especially valuable for individuals seeking careers in:

  • Development research
  • Human-centered design
  • Social innovation
  • Public health
  • Youth programming
  • International development

Click here for more global job opportunities

The post YLabs Youth Researcher Opportunity in Nigeria 2026 – Remote Field-Based Research Role for Early-Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Greenwich Postdoctoral Research Fellow Opportunity 2026 in the UK: Fully Engage in Global Research on Gender-Based Violence, Food Systems, and Social Justice
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The University of Greenwich has announced applications for an exciting Post Doctoral Research Fellow position within its internationally recognized Natural Resources Institute (NRI). This opportunity offers researchers the chance to contribute to groundbreaking work exploring Gender-Based Violence (GBV) within food systems through innovative, interdisciplinary, and socially impactful research. The position is based at the Medway […]

The post University of Greenwich Postdoctoral Research Fellow Opportunity 2026 in the UK: Fully Engage in Global Research on Gender-Based Violence, Food Systems, and Social Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Greenwich has announced applications for an exciting Post Doctoral Research Fellow position within its internationally recognized Natural Resources Institute (NRI). This opportunity offers researchers the chance to contribute to groundbreaking work exploring Gender-Based Violence (GBV) within food systems through innovative, interdisciplinary, and socially impactful research.

The position is based at the Medway Campus in the United Kingdom and provides a unique opportunity for researchers passionate about gender studies, food systems, participatory research, social justice, and sustainable development.

The successful candidate will join a globally respected research environment dedicated to addressing some of the world’s most pressing development challenges through collaborative and evidence-based approaches.

Applications close on May 20, 2026.

About the University of Greenwich and the Natural Resources Institute (NRI)

University of Greenwich is widely recognized as one of the United Kingdom’s leading institutions for impactful and socially engaged research. The university has built a strong reputation for advancing innovative research that contributes directly to policy development, sustainable development, and community transformation around the world.

Within the university, the Natural Resources Institute (NRI) serves as a specialist research institute focused on:

  • Food systems
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Livelihoods and institutions
  • Social justice
  • Climate resilience
  • International development

NRI collaborates extensively with partners across the Global South and supports evidence-driven initiatives that influence both policy and practice globally.

This postdoctoral role will be embedded within NRI’s dynamic interdisciplinary research environment, where researchers work closely with governments, civil society organizations, academic institutions, and local communities.

About the Research Project

Food systems around the world are increasingly recognized as spaces where gender inequalities strongly shape:

  • Labour conditions
  • Access to resources
  • Decision-making power
  • Economic opportunities
  • Exposure to violence

Despite growing global attention to gender equality, Gender-Based Violence (GBV) within food systems remains significantly under-researched.

This postdoctoral fellowship forms part of a pioneering transdisciplinary initiative that examines GBV across:

  • Agricultural labour systems
  • Food value chains
  • Contexts of food insecurity
  • Rural and marginalized communities

The research adopts:

  • Feminist approaches
  • Intersectional analysis
  • Decolonial perspectives
  • Creative and embodied methodologies
  • Audio-visual research techniques

The project aims to generate new evidence and creative outputs capable of informing:

  • Public policy
  • Development programming
  • Advocacy initiatives
  • Public awareness
  • Academic scholarship

Role Overview

The University of Greenwich is seeking a highly motivated Postdoctoral Research Fellow with expertise in one or more of the following areas:

  • Gender studies
  • Food systems research
  • Participatory methodologies
  • Creative research methods
  • Social justice research
  • Development studies

The selected researcher will contribute to several key activities throughout the project.

Main Responsibilities

1. Co-Producing Research Outputs

The researcher will help produce:

  • Audio-visual research materials
  • Written reports
  • Academic publications
  • Creative dissemination materials

2. Stakeholder Collaboration

The role involves co-designing research materials and outputs with:

  • Community stakeholders
  • International partners
  • Civil society actors
  • Research collaborators

3. International Research Collaboration

The successful candidate will work closely with project partners in:

  • Colombia
  • Nigeria

This provides valuable international exposure and collaborative research experience across multiple contexts.

4. Dissemination and Knowledge Sharing

The researcher will support the communication of findings to:

  • Policymakers
  • Academic audiences
  • Civil society organizations
  • Community actors
  • International development stakeholders

5. Academic Contributions

The role includes contributing to high-impact academic publications and interdisciplinary research outputs.

Salary and Contract Details

Salary

The annual salary for this position ranges from:

£38,784 to £44,746 per annum

The exact salary will depend on qualifications and experience.

Contract Type

  • Fixed-term contract
  • Duration: Until March 31, 2027

Work Location

  • Medway Campus
  • United Kingdom

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This position is particularly valuable for researchers seeking to:

  • Build an international academic career
  • Conduct socially impactful research
  • Engage with interdisciplinary methodologies
  • Collaborate with Global South partners
  • Influence policy and practice
  • Work within feminist and decolonial research frameworks

The opportunity also offers exposure to innovative creative research approaches, including audio-visual methodologies that are increasingly important in participatory and community-centered research.

Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion Commitment

The University of Greenwich strongly emphasizes equality, diversity, and inclusion in its recruitment and workplace culture.

The university particularly encourages applications from underrepresented groups, including:

  • Black communities
  • Asian communities
  • Minority ethnic groups
  • Disabled individuals
  • LGBT+ applicants

The institution is committed to:

  • Mental health support
  • Inclusive recruitment practices
  • Reasonable workplace adjustments
  • Diverse talent management
  • Employee wellbeing

The university also promotes a supportive and inclusive environment for researchers from diverse backgrounds and experiences.

How to Apply

Interested applicants must apply through the official University of Greenwich vacancies portal.

Applicants are required to:

  1. Complete the online application form
  2. Provide all required supporting statements
  3. Submit the application before the deadline

Important note:

  • Attached CVs and personal statements will not be reviewed at the initial application stage unless the required online sections are completed properly.

Applicants are therefore encouraged to carefully complete every section of the online application portal.

Important Dates

Application Release Date

May 6, 2026

Application Deadline

May 20, 2026

Interview Date

To be confirmed

Official Application Links

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post University of Greenwich Postdoctoral Research Fellow Opportunity 2026 in the UK: Fully Engage in Global Research on Gender-Based Violence, Food Systems, and Social Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Restless Development Uganda Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Remote Financial Capacity Building Consultancy for Youth-Led Organizations Funded by the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Restless Development is inviting qualified consultants and consultancy teams to apply for an important remote consultancy opportunity focused on strengthening the financial management systems and institutional capacity of youth-led organizations in Uganda. The consultancy forms part of the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective (AU-EU YVL-PoC), a major four-year initiative co-funded by […]

The post Restless Development Uganda Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Remote Financial Capacity Building Consultancy for Youth-Led Organizations Funded by the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Restless Development is inviting qualified consultants and consultancy teams to apply for an important remote consultancy opportunity focused on strengthening the financial management systems and institutional capacity of youth-led organizations in Uganda.

The consultancy forms part of the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective (AU-EU YVL-PoC), a major four-year initiative co-funded by the European Union to support youth participation, governance, and inclusive development across Africa and Europe.

This opportunity is ideal for experienced finance, accounting, compliance, and NGO management professionals with strong expertise in capacity building, financial systems strengthening, and organizational development for grassroots and youth-led organizations.

The consultancy will run for three months and offers a total budget of UGX 16,000,000 / €4,000, inclusive of taxes and professional fees.

Applications close on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at 5:00 PM (E.A.T).

 

About Restless Development

Restless Development Official Website is a globally recognized youth-led development agency committed to supporting young people to create a just, equitable, and sustainable world.

Since 1985, the organization has worked with thousands of young people globally to:

  • Promote youth leadership
  • Strengthen civic engagement
  • Advance climate justice
  • Improve livelihoods and education
  • Promote sexual and reproductive health rights
  • Encourage inclusive governance

Restless Development believes that young people are powerful agents of change and should be at the forefront of addressing local and global challenges.

The organization operates across multiple countries and works closely with youth-led organizations, governments, civil society actors, and development partners.

 

About the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective

The AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective (AU-EU YVL-PoC) is a four-year programme designed to build more inclusive societies in Africa and Europe by ensuring that youth voices are integrated into governance and policy-making processes.

The programme focuses on:

  • Youth participation in governance
  • Civic engagement
  • Leadership development
  • Advocacy skills
  • Institutional responsiveness to youth concerns

The initiative is implemented through a consortium of respected international organizations, including:

  • Oxfam
  • Restless Development
  • Search for Common Ground
  • Youthmakers Hub
  • Makesense Africa

The programme equips young people with the tools and skills needed to advocate for their priorities at:

  • Local levels
  • National levels
  • Regional levels
  • Continental platforms

It also works with institutions and policymakers to strengthen youth inclusion in governance systems.

 

Background of the Consultancy

As part of the programme implementation, 15 youth-led organizations were selected through a competitive funding process by the Youth Advisory Board and Restless Development.

Following selection:

  • Due diligence assessments were conducted
  • Organizational capacity gaps were identified
  • Capacity Building Plans (CBPs) were jointly developed with grantees

The assessments identified several institutional and financial management challenges requiring external technical support.

Some of the key identified needs include:

  • Weak internal control systems
  • Limited financial management tools
  • Gaps in procurement systems
  • Inadequate human resource policies
  • Weak compliance structures
  • Organizational governance challenges

This consultancy therefore seeks to provide technical assistance and tailored capacity-building support to selected grantees.

 

Objective of the Consultancy

The consultant will help strengthen the institutional and financial management capacity of prioritized youth-led organizations funded under the programme.

The main goal is to ensure that grantees:

  • Improve financial accountability
  • Strengthen compliance systems
  • Develop sustainable internal control mechanisms
  • Enhance institutional resilience
  • Meet donor financial reporting requirements

 

Key Responsibilities of the Consultant

The selected consultant or consultancy team will undertake several important tasks.

  1. Review Existing Organizational Systems

The consultant will review:

  • Due diligence reports
  • Capacity Building Plans (CBPs)
  • Organizational structures
  • Financial policies
  • Administrative systems
  • Human resource procedures
  • Procurement systems

This assessment will help determine the specific support required by each organization.

 

  1. Deliver Tailored Capacity Building Training

The consultant will:

  • Develop a training schedule
  • Conduct customized training sessions
  • Strengthen accountability mechanisms
  • Build sustainable financial management systems

The training should support the development of:

  • Democratic organizations
  • Legitimate governance structures
  • Sustainable institutional systems
  • Transparent financial processes

 

  1. Support Development of Internal Policies

The consultant will work directly with grantees to co-create and strengthen:

  • Financial policies
  • Procurement policies
  • Human resource policies
  • Internal control systems
  • Risk management frameworks

This support aims to improve organizational governance and operational efficiency.

 

Consultancy Duration and Budget

Duration

  • Three months

Engagement Mode

  • Fully remote/virtual

Budget

  • UGX 16,000,000
  • Equivalent to approximately €4,000
  • Inclusive of taxes and professional fees

 

Required Qualifications

Applicants should possess strong expertise in finance, compliance, and organizational development.

Educational Qualifications

Candidates should have:

  • An advanced degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field
  • CPA or ACCA certification is preferred

 

Professional Experience

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • At least 5–7 years of relevant professional experience
  • Experience managing EU-funded projects
  • Experience working with international NGOs
  • Proven expertise in financial management and compliance
  • Previous experience conducting organizational capacity-building assignments

 

Required Skills and Expertise

The ideal consultant should have:

  • Strong knowledge of internal controls and risk management
  • Experience supporting grassroots organizations
  • Change management expertise
  • Familiarity with youth-led programming
  • Experience working with NGOs and civil society organizations in Africa
  • Strong facilitation and training skills

 

Safeguarding Commitment

Restless Development places strong emphasis on safeguarding and the protection of:

  • Children
  • Young people
  • Vulnerable adults

The organization expects all consultants, staff, volunteers, and partners to comply fully with safeguarding standards and policies to ensure communities are protected from harm.

 

Why This Consultancy Matters

This consultancy provides a unique opportunity to contribute directly to strengthening youth-led organizations and improving institutional sustainability across Africa.

By supporting financial systems and governance structures, the selected consultant will help youth organizations:

  • Become more resilient
  • Improve donor compliance
  • Strengthen transparency
  • Scale their impact
  • Sustain long-term development initiatives

The consultancy also offers valuable experience working within an international consortium funded by the European Union.

 

How to Apply

Interested consultants should submit the following documents:

Required Application Documents

  1. Technical proposal outlining methodology and approach
  2. Financial proposal with a detailed budget in:
    • Uganda Shillings (UGX)
    • or Euros (€)
  3. Relevant portfolio and references
  4. CV(s) of consultancy team members
  5. Identification of a team lead

 

Important Dates

Deadline for Inquiries

Monday, May 18, 2026 at 12:00 PM (E.A.T)

Application Deadline

Thursday, May 21, 2026 at 5:00 PM (E.A.T)

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is highly encouraged.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Restless Development Uganda Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Remote Financial Capacity Building Consultancy for Youth-Led Organizations Funded by the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
